macro_segments.title: Transferring property to a fetus (עובר) and the foundational ruling that active transfer to an unborn child is invalid

micro_segments.title: Opening context: the prohibition on transferring property to a fetus, inheritance by a fetus, and the exception for one's own child
The case of a man gifting property to future children from a new marriage, and the son from the first marriage intervening
The son's kinyan to acquire a portion equal to the future children: does the kinyan stand even though the future children never acquired?

macro_segments.title: The three-against-three debate (Rabbi Abin et al. vs. Rabbi Abbahu et al.) on whether the son's kinyan remains valid when the future children did not acquire
Rabbi Yirmiyah's response to the age-based argument: compelling reasoning trumps seniority, and appeal to Rabbi Avin's concession
Rabbi Avin's explanation of why the son does not acquire: the principle of 'you and a donkey' (את וחמור) applied to conditional acquisitions
Expansion of את וחמור through parallels: the case of terumah from a spoiled gourd, and the five women kiddushin case showing את וחמור does work partially
Gifting to a wife and children: the meaning of 'to you and my children'—does it mean equal division or equal to one child
The dispute between Rav Yosef (fifty-fifty) and Abaye (equal to one child), with supporting cases and the final ruling
Cases of ambiguous designations: silk sent 'to the house,' property to 'my sons,' and property to 'my sons' with one son and one daughter
The question of whether 'sons' (בנים) can refer to a single son and whether it includes daughters or grandsons
The case of a man with a son and grandson: does 'my sons' include the grandson?
Capital improvements to an inherited estate: whether older sons' work on the undivided estate benefits all or only themselves
